channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Markets sink as Trump's tariffs roil global trading system
item.description: Global markets are plunging following last week’s two-day meltdown on Wall Street, and U.S. President Donald Trump says he won’t back down on his sweeping new tariffs, which have roiled global trade.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Live updates: Trump defends tariffs; Billionaire Trump endorser calls for trade war time-out
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are on defense amid the fallout from President Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked uncertainty about the economy. Markets took a nosedive Thursday following Trump’s announcement of a general 10 percent tariff on goods imported to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: The US has revoked visas for South Sudanese while civil war threatens at home
item.description: The United States once cheered the creation of South Sudan as an independent nation. Now the Trump administration has abruptly revoked the visas of all South Sudanese, saying the country’s government has failed to accept the return of its citizens “in a timely manner.
item.dc:creator: CARA ANNA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope makes surprise appearance at St. Peter’s Square, 2 weeks after leaving hospital
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis made a surprise entrance to St. Peter’s Square during a special Jubilee Mass for the sick and health workers on Sunday, marking his first public appearance at the Vatican since leaving t he hospital two weeks ago after a life-threatening bout with pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: SILVIA STELLACCI and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover carmaker pauses shipments to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: The company that makes Jaguar and Land Rover vehicles said on Saturday it would pause shipments to the U.S., as President Trump’s tariffs took effect this past week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Israel walks back its account of the killing of 15 medics in Gaza after video seems to contradict it
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The Israeli military backtracked on its account of the killing of 15 Palestinian medics by its forces last month after phone video appeared to contradict its claims that their vehicles did not have emergency signals on when troops opened fire on them in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, WAFAA SHURAFA and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill 32, mostly women and children
item.description: Palestinian health officials say Israeli strikes on the Gaza Strip have killed at least 32 people including over a dozen women and children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Reporter's Notebook: The Who... not too old to rock
item.description: Roger Daltrey and Pete Townshend lit up London in a rare, fiery performance for Teenage Cancer Trust — decades older, still unmistakably The Who.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yemen's Houthis say US strikes kill 6 as Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: Suspected U.S. airstrikes over the weekend targeting Yemen’s Houthi rebels killed at least six people.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Belgium charges 8 people in European Union Parliament bribery probe
item.description: Following a probe into suspected bribery at the European Parliament, eight people have been charged with money laundering, corruption and participation in a criminal organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN official reappointed despite accusations of antisemitism
item.description: Allegations of antisemitism and the condemnation of several nations did not stop Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese from securing another three-year term in her position.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New ecosystem found after Chicago-sized chunk of Antarctica breaks off
item.description: They found animal and marine life as deep as nearly 4,300 feet. The species in the newly discovered ecosystem include crustaceans, sponges, corals and octopus.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Singer Rara Sekar draws inspiration from nature, encourages people to return to simple living
item.description: OXFORD, England (AP) — Rara Sekar closed her eyes in meditation after performing a song that speaks of rays of light that cut through the fog as one political prisoner faced death more than six decades ago.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA and VICKRAM SOMBU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: England’s Wrestling Church seeks converts with baptisms and body slams
item.description: SHIPLEY, England (AP) — Sitting around a wrestling ring, churchgoers roared as local hero Billy O’Keeffe body-slammed a fighter named Disciple. Beneath stained-glass windows, they whooped and cheered as burly, tattooed wresters tumbled into the aisle during a six-man tag-team battle.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As stock market continues plummeting over tariffs, Trump spends the day at his golf course
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Two days after sending the economy reeling by announcing widespread tariffs, President Donald Trump insisted his trade policies will never change as he remained ensconced in a bubble of wealth and power in Florida.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, CHRIS MEGERIAN and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India's parliament passes bill that would change Muslim land endowments
item.description: India's parliament passes a bill amending Muslim land endowments, sparking protests over concerns it undermines minority rights and could lead to property confiscation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Comedian Russell Brand charged with rape and sexual assault in UK
item.description: British police on Friday charged Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault following an 18-month investigation sparked when four women alleged they had been assaulted by the controversial comedian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike on a school in Gaza kills at least 27 people, Palestinian health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 100 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including 27 or more sheltering at a school, according to Palestinian medical authorities, in a stepped-up offensive that Israel’s military said is intended to pressure Hamas and e
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: White House defends not including Russia, North Korea on tariffs
item.description: The official said that President Trump has “recently threatened to impose strong sanctions on Russia” to further explain leaving out Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs likely to spur recession: Moody's economist
item.description: Mark Zandi said the situation may not be as grave if countries do not respond “in kind” to the president’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will establish a new security corridor across Gaza to pressure Hamas
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Wednesday that Israel is establishing a new security corridor across the Gaza Strip to pressure Hamas, suggesting it would cut off the southern city of Rafah, which Israel has ordered evacuated, from the rest of the Palestinian
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and ISAAC SCHARF, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump unveils 'Liberation Day' tariffs, raising trade tensions
item.description: The tariffs goals include reducing the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. and boosting American-made manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Fourth US soldier missing in Lithuania found dead
item.description: A fourth American soldier who went missing after their vehicle became submerged in a bog during training in the country was found dead Tuesday, the White House announced.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9

item.title: Earthquake compounds Myanmar's humanitarian crisis as the death toll passes 2,000
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — The death toll in last week's massive earthquake in Myanmar has passed 2,000, state media said Monday, as accounts of some people's last moments emerged: Two hundred Buddhist monks crushed by a collapsing monastery. Fifty children killed when a preschool classroom crumbled.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel prepares to move deeper into Gaza and orders Rafah evacuated
item.description: The Israeli military indicated on Monday it could soon launch another major offensive in Rafah and ordered most of the southernmost city in the Gaza Strip evacuated. Israel ended its ceasefire with Hamas and renewed its air and ground war earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
